title: Accurate Detection of RNA Stem-Loops in Structurome Data Reveals Widespread Association with Protein Binding Sites
author: Pierce Radecki
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Supporting computational resources relevant to the manuscript: 

  Accurate Detection of RNA Stem-Loops in Structurome Data Reveals Widespread Association with Protein Binding Sites  

 Pierce Radecki, Rahul Uppuluri, Kaustubh Deshpande, Sharon Aviran 

 Biomedical Engineering Department and Genome Center, University of California, Davis 

 =========== CONTENT =========== 

 Enclosed in this repository are: 

 
	 STRAND dataset of representative RNA structures (diverse collection of non-redundant RNA structures as processed by CD-HIT) 
	 The Weeks set (curated set of 22 structured RNAs with high-quality SHAPE reactivity and reference structures) 
	 Python script to simulate artificial reactivities for a set of RNA structures 
 

   

 =========== DEPENDENCIES =========== 

 All code is written in Python 3 (tested using Python 3.7.9). 

 The following Python modules must be installed and available within your Python environment. 

 
	 Bio (Biopython module) 
	 matplotlib 
	 numpy 
	 re (regex) 
	 scipy 
	 sklearn 
 

 All modules can be installed using pip. 

  
=========== FILE STRUCTURE =========== 

 
	 Radecki_et_al_supplemental_code
	 
		 LICENSE 
		 README 
		 simulate_data.py 
		 files/
		 
			 STRAND (1191 non-redundant structures from RNA STRAND (processed with CD-HIT-EST) 
			 weeks_set (FASTA sequences, SHAPE reactivities, and reference structures (dot-bracket) for the Weeks set) 
		 
		 
		 libs/ (supporting files for the provided scripts)
		 
			 filelib.py 
			 rnalib.py 
			 simlib.py 
			 statlib.py 
		 
		 
		 simulated_data/
		 
			 STRAND/ (example simulated replicates of STRAND dataset)
			 
				 rep_1/ 
				 rep_2/ 
				 rep_3/ 
				 rep_4/ 
				 rep_5/ 
			 
			 
		 
		 
	 
	 
 

  
=========== USAGE AND EXAMPLES =========== 

 simulate_data.py 

 Script used to simulate artificial data for an input dataset according to Heitsch distributions. 

 Syntax: python simulate_data.py N_REPS output_directory 
- N_REPS: Number of replicates to generate for each scheme 
- output_directory: Output folder to save generated datasets for all schemes and replicates (will be created if 
    it does not exist) 

 Example: python simulate_data.py 10 simulated_data 

 For demonstration purposes, the input dataset used for generate the reactivities is hard-coded as the provided STRAND data.
